methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a public-stage company operating in Defense & Security. The valuation universe consists of 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. Across the Defense & Security sector, 21 same-sector peers exist, though the company operates in a structurally fragmented market with limited direct equivalents.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 21 same-sector peers, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow category. A fallback to all-sector median multiples would introduce material distortion given Kraken's specific market position and capital efficiency relative to broader Defense & Security benchmarks.

The limited comp set and absence of sufficient narrow-stage peers create meaningful constraints on triangulation. A complete valuation requires supplementary methods—discounted cash flow analysis anchored to Kraven's revenue trajectory, precedent transaction analysis within Defense & Security robotics, and tactical use of the 2 public tickers and 5 named private peers as indicative reference points rather than statistical medians. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, defense procurement cycles, and margin expansion timing. Further data collection on peer revenue multiples and growth rates is necessary before establishing a defensible valuation range.
